steps.title: Interpret the y-intercept
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is the graph's value when $x=0$. In context, $x=0$ means the moment the marble started moving, because $x$ measures seconds after the start. So the intercept describes the starting height, not a later time and not a minimum height.

steps.title: Read the starting height
steps.content: At $x=0$, the graph shows $y=8$. This means the marble's height was $8$ inches above the ground when it started moving. A statement about $0$ inches after $8$ seconds would describe a different point, and a statement about a minimum would describe a different feature of the graph. The answer is B.
